reasoning: Peer set: 12 helmet icons rendered in cool metallic pixel art — grays, silvers, one teal accent, one gold — with a consistent top-left light source, dark heavy outlines, and subdued highlights that read as polished metal. Charm register is gritty-functional, not magical. Candidate arrow: (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT — arrow has outlines but they're partially dissolved by the glow bloom; peers have crisp, uniform 1-2px black outlines — marginal fail. (2) OUTLINE COLOR — both dark, acceptable. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION — arrow shows a bottom-right cast shadow on the orb rim, peers use top-left lighting — minor inconsistency. (4) SHADOW COUNT — both ~1-2 layers, OK. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY — the arrow glows with a blazing warm-white core; peers have subtle specular dots. Extreme mismatch — fail. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE — arrow is warm orange/amber plus teal orb; peers are solidly cool (gunmetal, steel, one teal helm, one yellow-gold). No warm-orange item exists in the peer sheet — fail. (7) DETAIL DENSITY — arrow body is simpler than helmets, but that is item-type difference — borderline acceptable. (8) PROPORTION — comparable icon footprint, pass. (9) RENDERING STYLE — both pixel art, but arrow uses a 'magic glow' visual language (radial gradient bloom, soft anti-alias on orb) that reads as a different sub-style from the clean hard-edge pixel helmets — marginal fail. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP — peer palette: near-black, mid-gray, light-gray, silver, white-spec, teal, gold. Candidate palette: bright orange, warm yellow, teal-cyan, off-white bloom. Overlap is at most the teal tone; fail. (11) CHARM LEVEL — peers are stoic/gritty armour pieces; candidate radiates magical-elemental energy with its orb presentation. Charm register diverges significantly — fail. Summary: 5 of 11 dimensions fail outright, 2 marginal. Palette and color-temperature drift are the hardest issues to reconcile without a redesign.
